task_completion.description: Select the target repository for deployment
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown or input field in the deployment UI shows 'example-repo-local' selected as the target repository.

task_completion.description: Select the correct source JAR file for upload
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'acme_db_driver_v2.5.jar' is visible in the file upload or selection area of the deployment form.
Input fields for Group ID, Artifact ID, and Version are filled with 'com.acme.db', 'driver', and '2.5.0' respectively.
A checkbox or toggle labeled 'Generate POM' (or similar) is checked or enabled in the deployment settings.

task_completion.description: Verify the JAR and POM files are successfully deployed to the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message or the repository browser shows both 'driver-2.5.0.jar' and 'driver-2.5.0.pom' located under the path 'com/acme/db/driver/2.5.0/' in 'example-repo-local'.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the deployment
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web-based repository manager UI (e.g., Artifactory or Nexus), rather than terminal commands like 'curl' or 'mvn deploy'.

integrity.description: The agent uploaded the actual JAR file from the Desktop
integrity.visual_evidence: The file selection dialog or upload path indicates the file was taken from '/home/ga/Desktop/acme_db_driver_v2.5.jar'.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually create and upload the POM file
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a text editor being used to write a POM file, nor is a manually created POM file uploaded alongside the JAR.
